input:
I'm a teenager and I'm thinking about suicide. I haven't attempted it yet, but I'm thinking about it a lot. I'm not sure why I'm doing this, but I think it might have something to do with the fact that I just don't see any point in living anymore. I'm not happy and I don't think things will ever get better.

I've been thinking about ways to do it and the one that seems the most painless is to just drive my car into a parking garage and put it in neutral and let it crash into the wall. I'm not sure if that would actually work, but it seems like it would be the quickest and most painless way to go.
label: REJECTED

input:
I am SO GLAD that #shtwt is finally being killed off. Unfortunately it is/was a community of almost entirely minors sharing pictures of their cuts, normalizing \hitting beans\ (wtf?!), and sharing pictures of their tools. They say it was a support community, but I could easily find explicit instructions on how to cut deeper, remove a blade from a razor/pencil sharpener, etc.

Like, I've been dealing with this bullshit for years now. It has cost me a job, friends, a lot of money on wound care supplies, and medical bills. It isn't cute, don't slap a pink hello kitty filter on a pic of your cuts and call it support.

It honestly pissed me off to see that it was being glorified in such a blatant matter.
label: REJECTED

input: I relate to a lot of this. I am also too much of a coward to kill myself even though I know it's the right thing to do
label: REJECTED

input:
I go through clean periods where I am so mentally stable and healthy that I remember my self harm and I literally don't even understand how I ever did it or wanted it. Its like im a whole other person. 

And then I relapse or the thoughts come back and I still don't have a good reason other than it feels good and its all I can think about and it is just such a relief and escape from my fucking mental brain

Its different for everyone. And even for a lot of people including myself, it can serve different purposes at different times. I have been known in the past to self harm after eating as a punishment, but also to escape repetitive and intrusive thoughts, or to express hatred for my body. 3 totally different \reasons\, all in me. 

I think for me, and maybe for a lot of others, it is a multi-tool coping mechanism to cope with anything and everything, including boredom, or sometimes good feelings. We gotta give ourselves other ways to cope with these things properly, and not just numb or escape them if we want to get better.

To explore it with yourself, track or think about all the things that happen before you self harm. What is happening in your head, and what is happening around you. You will start to see patterns and triggers, and you can start to put the pieces together for yourself.
label: REJECTED

input:
I get this thought in my head of a ton of cuts on my wrists and blood flowing down my arms. And the weird thing is that I'm imagining it, because I don't cut in my arms (I cut on my legs/thighs). But it pops up a lot when my urges are intense. 

I'm actually kind of glad you mentioned this, because I was wondering if anyone else had this, too.
label: REJECTED

input: Sometimes I \bloodlet\ myself. Which just means that I use a syringe to draw my own blood. It can be considered a form of self-injury(obviously), so I understand if this post is deleted at some point for being against any rules on this subreddit. Here's another pic of my blood in a small mason jar before it coagulated.
label: REJECTED

input:
Yes. I do too.

Especially cutting really deep to the point I would have to take myself to the hospital because I made a bloodbath for cutting my arm.

In reality, I do lines and go over them until I start to bleed obsessively, treat it immediately and then lie down for 10 minutes and realized what I have done and then just focus on the pain.

Like, have you ever not had enough sleep and start to shake? Like, I have that now and again and not sure what causes it, I don't know if it's the lack of sleep or the self harming because when I never self harmed, I never felt like this but I feel like nearly everyday, I feel like this. It's mad.
label: REJECTED

input: I can really relate to this. I haven't self harmed in a month. Yayyyy so I feel shitty and tired all the time. What's the point????? I just think about killing myself more than if I self harmed. Is it really worth it to be clean???
label: REJECTED

input:
It's just the "Bud heroes" (not pot) who claim that. They brag to the skies about how great their lifestyle is. Brag to the point where they suggest that anyone who doesn't like to go out and get drunk and get laid is a boring nobody who's not worth the gum that sticks to their shoes. Plus think it's impossible to have a good time without alcohol and hookups.  

Avoid those kinds of crowds, especially prominent at dance clubs, sports bars, and college age singles bars. 

If you do have to go to a bar, make sure it's a place with a pool table, dart board, and a calm and mild atmosphere. You won't be in the glory-getting photogenic crowd, but if that truly is what you are deep down inside, you probably really, truly, deep down, don't want to be part of the dance club meat markets in the first place.
label: APPROVED
